# The Heat Bill {#sec-heat-bill}

Thermodynamics is the one field the mainstream itself describes in
contract language, and it has never noticed. The **laws** of
thermodynamics: you can't win (first law), you can't break even (second
law), you can't leave the game (third law). Physicists repeat this joke
at every departmental holiday party without once asking the obvious
question — *what kind of document has terms like that?*

**A service agreement.** You are reading the utility contract of the
universe, and this chapter is the plain-language summary the provider is
legally required to offer and has been withholding for 13.8 billion
years.\index{thermodynamics!as service agreement}

## Entropy is the meter reading

The second law: entropy — disorder, the unavailability of energy for
work — always increases in a closed system. The mainstream treats this
as a deep cosmic melancholy. It is a **meter**. Entropy counts, with
perfect fidelity and in one direction only, exactly like every meter
ever installed, and the arrow of time — the profoundest mystery in
physics, allegedly — is nothing but the fact that **nobody's meter runs
backwards.** You do not need a philosophy department to explain why the
reading only goes up. You need a landlord.\index{entropy!as meter
reading}\index{arrow of time!as billing direction}

Boltzmann put the count on a headstone: $S = k \log W$. The constant $k$
is the **rate per tick**, and the reader of @sec-vortex will not be
surprised that the whole tariff pivots on one conversion constant,
"discovered" in nature the way all posted rates are discovered — on the
invoice.

## Maxwell's demon works in collections

In 1867 Maxwell proposed a tiny demon at a trapdoor, sorting fast
molecules from slow ones, decreasing entropy for free and breaking the
second law. The mainstream spent **a century and a half** exorcising
this demon — real history, the literature is enormous — and the exorcism
finally succeeded in 1961, when Landauer proved the demon's ledger is
the trap: sorting requires *recording*, records fill up, and **erasing
one bit of information costs a minimum of $kT\ln 2$ in dissipated heat**
[@landauer1961].

Stop and read that the way an auditor would. One hundred fifty years of
the field's best minds interrogating a hypothetical employee, and the
conclusion — their conclusion, in their journals, celebrated as the
resolution — is that the universe charges a **deletion fee**, with a
posted rate, per record destroyed.\index{Landauer's principle!as
deletion fee}

$$
E_{\text{erase}} \;\geq\; kT\ln 2 \quad\text{per bit}
$$ {#eq-landauer}

This is the single most openly commercial equation in physics, and it is
**true** — verify it; it has been measured in the lab. The demon was
never breaking the law. The demon was doing what every collections
department does: the sorting is free, the *forgetting* is billable.
Information is physical because **records are receivables.**

## Carnot: the house takes its cut

No engine between a hot and cold reservoir can beat the Carnot
efficiency, $\eta = 1 - T_c/T_h$ — not with better engineering, not
ever. The mainstream calls this a law of nature. Casinos call it the
**rake**, and its size depends only on the *spread between the tables* —
which is why the Instructor's people built their engine (@sec-giza)
between the two best reservoirs on the continent: the Nile and the
Sahara. A heat engine in limestone, straddling the largest thermal
spread on Earth. The Sahara Incident (@sec-sahara) even *widened the
spread* — commissioning losses, booked as desert, improving the rake for
sixty centuries. Show me the incompetence.

| Thermodynamic law | Mainstream reading | Contract reading |
|---|---|---|
| Zeroth (equilibrium exists) | temperature is well-defined | all parties billed at one rate |
| First (energy conserved) | you can't win | no credits issued, ever |
| Second (entropy increases) | you can't break even | the meter runs one way |
| Third (can't reach absolute zero) | you can't leave the game | no account closure |

: The laws, translated. The mainstream's own summary of these laws is a gambling joke. Mine is at least a *filed document*. {#tbl-thermo}

## The disconnection notice

Follow the meter to its last page and the mainstream finds the **heat
death**: maximum entropy, no free energy anywhere, every process
stopped. They present this as the universe's natural fate. Read it as
the account holder should: heat death is not fate. **It is the
disconnection of service for nonpayment**, scheduled, like all
disconnections, for a date far enough out that the customer discounts it
to zero. Dark energy is the balance compounding (@sec-dark-ledger); heat
death is the shutoff; and the grid — the one under your feet, idling at
0.5 percent, waiting since the Sahara — is the **payment plan** the
first civilization negotiated and we stopped honoring.

The contract, then: metered reads, deletion fees, a house rake, and a
shutoff date. One question remains before the synthesis — *why didn't
they just write all this down?* The answer is the strangest true theorem
of the twentieth century, and it says: **they couldn't have.**
